english: natives were inclined to give to our European bean. - sran-púṅ (sran phung) a heap of pease, sran-púb (sran phub) pease-straw, sran-pýe (sran phye) flour of pease, sran-mé (sran me) blossom of pease. - 2. grain, like rdóg-po (rdog po), e.g. of Indian corn; even lcágs-kyi sran-čuṅ (lcags kyi sran chung) Wdn. grains of shot(?).

bridle, rtaí (rta'i); srab sga stan tsáṅ-po (srab sga stan gtsang po) a complete riding-gear; *srab čug-cè* (*srab bcug ce*) W. to bridle, to bit (a horse), *srab gyúr-če* (*srab sgyur ce*) to govern, to rein (a horse) srab-skyógs (srab skyogs) Cs. the reins, - srab-lcags (srab lcags) Cs. the bit. - srab-mtúr (srab mthur) Sch. the halter. - srab-mdá (srab mda') Stg., Ld.: *sram-dá* (*sram da'*) reins.

B., *sráb-mo* (*srab mo*) W. thin, tender, fine, e.g. skin Dzl., cloth, leather, paper, clouds; shallow, loose, not close; srab-mtil (srab mthil) Sch. inner sole, welt; srab-mtúg (srab mthug) thickness, dimension.

1. otter, the flesh of which is considered very nourishing, the liver is used as a remedy for strangury S g., but encountering this animal is regarded as an evil omen S.g.; ču-sram (chu sram) id.? (Cs. beaver?) nyá-sram (nya sram) Mil., either the same, or: fishes and otters; brag-srám (brag sram) rock-otter? sable? *ka-lon-sram* (*ka lon sram*) W. prob. sable; it is nearly black and stated to live near Yarkand, in the mountains as well as in the flat country. The ear-coverings worn by the ladies of Ladak are made of the fur of this animal. - 2. otter-skin, sable-skin.

resp. for bu (bu), son, child, dpon-srás (dpon sras), rgyal-srás (rgyal sras) son of the sovereign, a prince; rgyal-srás (rgyal sras) also: son of Buddha, a Buddha; lha(i) sras(-po) (lha(i) sras(-po)) 1. son of a god Dzl. 2. a prince; tugs-srás (thugs sras) spiritual son or daughter Mil.; in this sense sras (sras) may be applied to females: saṅs-rgyás-kyi sras dág-pao (sangs rgyas kyi sras dag pao) she has become a spotless child of Buddha Dzl. - srás-bu (sras bu) = sras (sras). - srás-mo (sras mo) daughter, young lady, princess. - sras-tsáb (sras tshab) Cs. adopted child.

1. a species of devil or demon, devouring esp. children, a vampire, also sri-ṅán (sri ngan) Schl., čuṅ-sri (chung sri) Glr., puṅ-sri (phung sri) Mil. a devil bringing misfortune; they are supposed to live in underground places, and are therefore also called más-kyi sri (mas kyi sri); sri laṅ (sri lang) a devil rises from below; sri nón-pa (sri gnon pa) B., *nán-če* (*gnan ce*) W. to lay, suppress a devil.

I. pf. bsris (bsris), fut. bsri (bsri), 1. to retain, e. g. bšaṅ-ba (bshang ba), gcin (gcin) constipation, strangury. - 2. to be parsimonious, niggardly, esp. with nor (nor); sba-sri-méd-par gnáṅ-ba (sba sri med par gnang ba) Mil. to give unsparingly, to bestow very liberally; *sri-šes-kán* (*sri shes mkhan*) W. parsimonious. II. W. to wind, to wrap round, for dkri-ba (dkri ba).
less frq. srid-žu (srid zhu), respect, reverence, deference, sri-žu-pa (sri zhu pa), sri-žu-mkan (sri zhu mkhan) one paying his regards, his respects, showing deference.

pf. bsriṅs (bsrings), fus. bsriṅ (bsring) 1. (cognate to riṅ-ba (ring ba)) to extend, stretch, stretch out, the arm, to hand, reach, *de dul soṅ, ṅá-la sriṅ toṅ* (*de 'dul song, nga la sring tong*) it has fallen down, hand it to me W.; to fling far away C. - 2. to postpone, put off, ‛či-bai tse ('chi ba'i tshe) Glr. the term of death; to prolong, tse (tshe) life S.g.; to wait, to tarry, *‛á-tsig sriṅ* (*a tshig sring*) Lh. wait a little, *dag-sa yoṅ-ṅa tsa-big sriṅ-te yoṅ?* (*bdag sa yong nga tsha big sring te yong?*) shall (I, you, he etc.) come directly or after a while? Ld.; *nam di r sriṅ-ce ča dug?* (*nam 'dir sring ce cha dug?*) how long shall you stay here? W. - 3. to send (skúr-ba (skur ba) Lex.) prin (phrin), yo-byád (yo byad) Sch. - 4. skyéd-sriṅ-ba (skyed sring ba) to bring up, train up, to rear Glr.
sister (of a male person, cf. miṅ-po (ming po)) bu-sriṅ (bu sring), miṅ-sriṅ (ming sring), resp. lcam-sriṅ (lcam sring) brother and sister, cousins.

1. length, extension, phug srid-du kru-bco-brgyád-pa (phug srid du khru bco brgyad pa) a cavern 18 cubits long Tar.; more frq. with regard to time: ‛di ('di) or de-srid(-kyi bar)-du (de srid (-kyi bar) -du) (for) so long (a time), ci-srid-du (ci srid du), also ci-srid-de (ci srid de), how long (a time)? also: as long as; when followed by yan (yan): be it ever so long (in this case ji (ji) would be more correct); also srid-par (srid par), or srid (srid) alone, for srid-du (srid du). - 2. dominion, government, srid-la ma ‛cám-pas (srid la ma 'cham pas) falling out with one another about the government Glr., esp. rgyal-srid (rgyal srid), dbaṅ-srid (dbang srid) id.; srid byéd-pa (srid byed pa) to reign, to govern, srid ‛tso-ba (srid 'tsho ba) id. Dzl.; ‛di gnyis-kyi srid gaṅ yód-rnams ('di gnyis kyi srid gang yod rnams) p'rog (phrog) he
